steps.title: Recognize that population change depends on density change
steps.content:
Since Population = Density × Area, and Iceland's land area is constant at 100,250 km², the population increase equals (change in density) × (constant area).

Change in density: $3.3 - 2.5 = 0.8$ people per km²

steps.title: Multiply the change in density by the constant area
steps.content:
Population increase = $$0.8 \times 100,250 = 80,200 \text{ people}$$

This matches choice D — D is correct.
